title: Exact Duplicates, Boilerplate, Similarity, Canonicals, and Change Detection · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds content identity and change-evidence model from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of content identity and change-evidence model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Keep response-byte, decoded-source, normalized-text, extracted-record, and report hashes distinct and version their transformations.
Separate repeated navigation, templates, legal text, and page-specific main content with site-tested rules and retained raw evidence.
Use documented tokenization, fingerprints, thresholds, and labeled evaluation and report uncertain similarity without claiming semantic equality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Exact Byte Text Hashes
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 9866 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate byte, source, text, record, canonical, similarity, boilerplate, and change evidence; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-change-classification-ut9xry
# Competency: scraper-change-classification.
# Case 7388: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_change_classification_ut9xry(fetched_pages: int = 80, exact_groups: int = 7, near_groups: int = 5, changed_pages: int = 18, unsupported_equivalence_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ut9xry = "ut9xry-yrx9tu-7388"
    assert evidence_variant_ut9xry
    evidence_axes_ut9xry = (
        "authorizationut9xry",
        "url7388",
        "responseyrx9tu",
        "treeut9xry7388",
        "frontier7388ut9xry",
        "reportyrx9tu7388",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ut9xry)) == 6
    if fetched_pages < 1 or exact_groups < 0 or near_groups < 0:
        return False, fetched_pages, "dedup-count"
    if changed_pages < 0 or changed_pages > fetched_pages:
        return False, changed_pages, "change-count"
    if unsupported_equivalence_claims > 0:
        return False, unsupported_equivalence_claims, "similarity-overclaim"
    if exact_groups + near_groups < 2:
        return False, exact_groups + near_groups, "dedup-evidence"
    assert changed_pages <= fetched_pages and unsupported_equivalence_claims == 0
    return True, changed_pages, "bounded-content-identity"
